records.ety: [OE. bocler, OF. bocler, F. bouclier, a shield with a boss, from OF. bocle, boucle, boss. See Buckle, n.]

defs: A kind of shield, of various shapes and sizes, worn on one of the arms (usually the left) for protecting the front of the body.
notes: ☞ In the sword and buckler play of the Middle Ages in England, the buckler was a small shield, used, not to cover the body, but to stop or parry blows.

defs: One of the large, bony, external plates found on many ganoid fishes.
The anterior segment of the shell of trilobites.
A block of wood or plate of iron made to fit a hawse hole, or the circular opening in a half-port, to prevent water from entering when the vessel pitches.

quotes: Can Oxford, that did ever fence the right, Now buckler falsehood with a pedigree?
